iti105:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti105:2.1: “Monks, there are these four birthplaces of craving where a monk’s craving, when taking birth, takes birth.
iti105:2.3: Either for the sake of cloth a monk’s craving, when taking birth, takes birth.
iti105:2.4: Or for the sake of alms food a monk’s craving, when taking birth, takes birth.
iti105:2.5: Or for the sake of lodging a monk’s craving, when taking birth, takes birth.
iti105:2.6: Or for the sake of becoming or not becoming this or that a monk’s craving, when taking birth, takes birth.
iti105:2.7: These are the four birthplaces of craving where a monk’s craving, when taking birth, takes birth.”
iti105:3.1: With craving his companion, a man
iti105:3.2: wanders on a long, long time.
iti105:3.3: Neither in this state here nor anywhere else
iti105:3.4: does he go beyond the wandering- on.
iti105:4.2: that craving brings stress into play–
iti105:4.3: free from craving, devoid of clinging,
iti105:4.4: mindful, the monk lives the mendicant life.
